--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: e780
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Card: spatial nav into card sub-parts (tags, assignees, title, fields on a card)'
+---
+## What
+
+Cards in the board view show a title plus zero or more sub-parts — tag pills, assignee pills, due dates, etc. The spatial nav system should let the user step INTO a card's sub-parts, not just between cards.
+
+Today (verified this session):
+- Clicking a tag pill inside a card sets focus on the tag moniker (e.g. `tag:01KN9K546V...`).
+- Clicking the card's body sets focus on the task moniker.
+- But there is no h/j/k/l path from a focused card to its internal tag/assignee pills; the user has no way to navigate into a card's interior.
+
+### Open design questions (resolve in this task)
+
+1. What key enters a card? `Enter` is already Inspector-open. Does `l` (right) from a card enter its first sub-part? Or does entering require an explicit command like `card.focusInside`?
+2. Do sub-parts participate in the global spatial beam test, or only when their parent card is focused (to avoid every tag pill on the board competing with cards during beam tests)?
+3. What does `h` / ` j` / `k` do while a sub-part is focused? Stay inside the card's sub-parts, or bubble out?
+
+Decide with the user before writing code. Good starting point:
+
+- Sub-parts register spatial rects only when inside the focused card (conditional registration is a refactor; simpler: always register, but the beam test prefers candidates with the same parent scope — container-first search). Already built into Rust `navigate`.
+- `Enter` opens inspector. `l` on a card with sub-parts moves to first sub-part. `h` on a sub-part at its leftmost edge moves back to the card.
+
+### TDD — failing tests (after design is resolved)
+
+```ts
+describe("card interior navigation", () => {
+  it("l from a focused card moves focus to the first tag pill on that card", async () => {
+    await click(cardEl("card-1-1"));
+    await expectFocused("task:card-1-1");
+    await keyboard("l");
+    // First tag on that card, e.g. "tag:bug"
+    const focused = await getFocusedMoniker();
+    expect(focused).toMatch(/^tag:/);
+  });
+
+  it("h from a pill back to the card body", async () => { ... });
+
+  it("j from a card does NOT reach pills on a different card", async () => {
+    // Container-first search keeps nav inside the focused card's sub-parts.
+  });
+});
+```
+
+### Approach
+
+1. Confirm current pill registration: do tag pills inside cards already call `FocusScope` today? If yes, they're already spatial candidates and container-first search might work if we pass `parent_scope`. If no, wrap them.
+2. If pills already have FocusScopes, the likely gap is `parent_scope` isn't being threaded through `spatial_register` — React passes `parent_scope: null` everywhere today. Thread it.
+3. Verify Rust `SpatialState::navigate` container-first logic picks siblings-with-same-parent-scope before fanning out.
+
+### Acceptance
+
+- [ ] Design decision documented in the task body (update description)
+- [ ] Failing E2E for each decided behavior
+- [ ] Implementation passes tests
+- [ ] No regression in card-to-card board nav (sibling task)
\ No newline at end of file
